sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(88) — Martis Lake tributaries (Nevada and Placer Cos.)
USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Reservoir layer record for gaz_id 1655186, "Martis Creek Lake", Reservoir, Nevada/Placer County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":1655186,"gaz_name":"Martis Creek Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Reservoir","state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Nevada/Placer"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13371036.1314, 4767642.0772) converts to lon -120.114061, lat 39.320202 — this is the officially named GNIS feature for the reservoir commonly called "Martis Lake"/"Martis Creek Lake", used as the anchor coordinate for these tributary mouths. The file's prior coordinate (-120.0980, 39.3050) was ~2.18km away and unsupported by any GNIS-backed source; corrected to lon -120.1141, lat 39.3202, gnisId 1655186.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: (88) Martis Lake tributaries (Nevada and Placer cos.). All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout.

sources.title: Martis Creek Lake | U.S. Army Corps of Engineers
sources.quotedText: Martis Creek Lake was the first "catch and release trophy trout" lake established in California. Varieties include Rainbow, Brown and Lahonton Cutthroat trout. Anglers must use flies, barbless hooks and artificial lures only. Live bait is not permitted. No fishing is allowed in the streams above the lake.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
